CheckTitle: Conditional Access policy enforces Token Protection
Description: At least one enabled Conditional Access policy should enforce **Token Protection** (the **secureSignInSession** session control) for targeted users. Token Protection binds sign-in session tokens (such as Primary Refresh Tokens) to the device, reducing the effectiveness of token theft and replay attacks.
Risk: Without **Token Protection**, stolen sign-in tokens can be replayed from an attacker's device to impersonate the user and bypass authentication, including **MFA**, because the token is not cryptographically bound to the original device.

Code.Other:
1. Navigate to the Microsoft Entra admin center at https://entra.microsoft.com/
2. Go to **Protection** > **Conditional Access** > **Policies** > **New policy**
3. Under **Users**, include the targeted users (exclude only documented break-glass accounts)
4. Under **Target resources**, select the supported applications (e.g., Exchange Online, SharePoint Online)
5. Under **Conditions** > **Device platforms**, include Windows
6. Under **Session**, enable **Require token protection for sign-in sessions**
7. Set the policy to **On** and click **Create**

Recommendation.Text: Create a Conditional Access policy that enforces Token Protection (secure sign-in session) for supported applications on Windows devices to mitigate token theft and replay.
